initial_description:
The diagram illustrates the hardware-aware model selection process using llmfit, flowing from top to bottom. The background is a very light pastel blue.

The diagram is divided into five major sections, each represented by a rectangular box with rounded corners and a thin, dark gray border. Each box has a title in bold, dark gray text, centered at the top.

**Step 1 — Hardware Probe:** This box is at the top. Inside, the text "probe_hardware tool calls llmfit CLI" is displayed in a smaller, regular font. Below this, a stylized computer icon (light gray fill, dark gray outline) is shown, with radiating lines suggesting system probing. Underneath the icon, the text "CPU model and core count, total RAM (GB), GPU/NPU VRAM (GB), accelerator type, OS platform" is listed, separated by commas. Below this is an example output in a monospaced font: "Example: Apple M4 Max: 14 cores, 36 GB RAM, 36 GB VRAM, Metal GPU".

An arrow (dark gray, medium thickness) points downwards from the bottom center of the "Hardware Probe" box to the top center of the next box.

**Step 2 — Capability Scoring:** This box is below the first. Inside, the text "llmfit scores each candidate model against the hardware profile" is displayed in a smaller, regular font. Below this, a stylized scoring icon (three bars of increasing height, light gray fill, dark gray outline) is shown. Underneath the icon, the text "Model parameter count vs available VRAM, quantization level (Q4/Q8/F16), context window requirements, inference speed estimate (tok/s)" is listed, separated by commas. Models are ranked by a composite fit score (0–100).

An arrow (dark gray, medium thickness) points downwards from the bottom center of the "Capability Scoring" box to the top center of the next box.

**Step 3 — Ranked Recommendations:** This box is below the second. Inside, the text "The system produces a ranked list with fit scores" is displayed in a smaller, regular font. Below this, a bulleted list shows example ranked recommendations in a monospaced font:
* "qwen3.5:latest → score 94 (fits in VRAM, fast inference)"
* "qwen3-coder:30b → score 81 (fits with Q4 quantization)"
* "qwen2.5-coder:latest → score 78 (lighter, very fast)"
* "llava:34b → score 52 (marginal VRAM fit)"
* "mixtral:8x7b → score 31 (too large for available VRAM)"

An arrow (dark gray, medium thickness) points downwards from the bottom center of the "Ranked Recommendations" box to the top center of the next box.

**Step 4 — Auto-Selection & Agent Initialization:** This box is below the third. Inside, the text "The highest-scoring available model is auto-selected" is displayed in a smaller, regular font. Below this, the text "The agent is initialized with create_agent(model=selected_model)" is displayed. Below this, the text "Selected model displayed in Web UI sidebar footer" is displayed. Finally, "Users can override by setting LLM_MODEL in .env" is displayed.

An arrow (dark gray, medium thickness) points downwards from the bottom center of the "Auto-Selection & Agent Initialization" box to the top center of the next box.

**Step 5 — Model Pull (if needed):** This box is at the bottom. Inside, the text "If the selected model is not yet downloaded, the agent triggers ollama pull <model> automatically before starting inference" is displayed in a smaller, regular font. Below this, the text "Pull progress is streamed via SSE to the Models view in the UI" is displayed. A stylized download icon (arrow pointing downwards into a box, light gray fill, dark gray outline) is included.
optimized_description:
The diagram illustrates the hardware-aware model selection process using llmfit, flowing from top to bottom. The background is a very light pastel blue.

The diagram is divided into five major sections, each represented by a rectangular box with rounded corners and a thin, soft gray border. Each box has a title in bold, dark gray sans-serif text, centered at the top. The boxes themselves have a very light cream fill.

**Step 1 — Hardware Probe:** This box is at the top. Inside, the text "probe_hardware tool calls llmfit CLI" is displayed in a smaller, regular sans-serif font. Below this, a stylized computer icon (light gray fill, soft gray outline) is shown, with radiating lines suggesting system probing. Underneath the icon, the text "CPU model and core count, total RAM (GB), GPU/NPU VRAM (GB), accelerator type, OS platform" is listed, separated by commas, in a regular sans-serif font. Below this is an example output in a monospaced font: "Example: Apple M4 Max: 14 cores, 36 GB RAM, 36 GB VRAM, Metal GPU".

An arrow (soft gray, medium thickness) points downwards from the bottom center of the "Hardware Probe" box to the top center of the next box.

**Step 2 — Capability Scoring:** This box is below the first. Inside, the text "llmfit scores each candidate model against the hardware profile" is displayed in a smaller, regular sans-serif font. Below this, a stylized scoring icon (three bars of increasing height, light gray fill, soft gray outline) is shown. Underneath the icon, the text "Model parameter count vs available VRAM, quantization level (Q4/Q8/F16), context window requirements, inference speed estimate (tok/s)" is listed, separated by commas, in a regular sans-serif font. Models are ranked by a composite fit score (0–100).

An arrow (soft gray, medium thickness) points downwards from the bottom center of the "Capability Scoring" box to the top center of the next box.

**Step 3 — Ranked Recommendations:** This box is below the second. Inside, the text "The system produces a ranked list with fit scores" is displayed in a smaller, regular sans-serif font. Below this, a bulleted list shows example ranked recommendations in a monospaced font:
* "qwen3.5:latest → score 94 (fits in VRAM, fast inference)"
* "qwen3-coder:30b → score 81 (fits with Q4 quantization)"
* "qwen2.5-coder:latest → score 78 (lighter, very fast)"
* "llava:34b → score 52 (marginal VRAM fit)"
* "mixtral:8x7b → score 31 (too large for available VRAM)"

An arrow (soft gray, medium thickness) points downwards from the bottom center of the "Ranked Recommendations" box to the top center of the next box.

**Step 4 — Auto-Selection & Agent Initialization:** This box is below the third. Inside, the text "The highest-scoring available model is auto-selected" is displayed in a smaller, regular sans-serif font. Below this, the text "The agent is initialized with create_agent(model=selected_model)" is displayed in a regular sans-serif font. Below this, the text "Selected model displayed in Web UI sidebar footer" is displayed in a regular sans-serif font. Finally, "Users can override by setting LLM_MODEL in .env" is displayed in a regular sans-serif font.

An arrow (soft gray, medium thickness) points downwards from the bottom center of the "Auto-Selection & Agent Initialization" box to the top center of the next box.

**Step 5 — Model Pull (if needed):** This box is at the bottom. Inside, the text "If the selected model is not yet downloaded, the agent triggers ollama pull <model> automatically before starting inference" is displayed in a smaller, regular sans-serif font. Below this, the text "Pull progress is streamed via SSE to the Models view in the UI" is displayed in a regular sans-serif font. A stylized download icon (arrow pointing downwards into a box, light gray fill, soft gray outline) is included.
